pages.full_text:
On + we picked black-berries at lunch time. Simon sat in the sun.
On arrival at [?Flatter] __Malestroit__ municipal campsite * * * * *
we wandered into town on our bikes, chained them infront of the church, looked aroun[d] the town. + shopped.

[Glued-in photo: standing stones at Carnac at sunset, captioned "ALIGNEMENTS DE CARNAC"]
[Glued-in ticket: CARNAC, Alignements de / VCI TR CARNAC VISITE CONF INDIV / VCI Adulte - Anglais / le 26/08 a 11:30 / Menec ouest / Valable le 26/08/2014 / 5.00 EUR / Vendu le 26/08/2014 a 11:03 NI ECHANGE NI REMBOURSE / C213/PATI 240690052393]

Simon bought a purse, Eve bought gloves. We went back to the van (over the bridge across the river) + returned to town to have supper at the Grain of Salt. At 8.30. we went to a fabulous organ concert in the Church by Prof Mickael Gaborieau. Really amazing. There was a video link so we could see his hands, feet + the music clearly.
pages.notes: Top of page is partially cropped — text above ticket begins mid-sentence. The word before 'Malestroit' is unclear; could be a place name like 'Flatters' or an adjective. Ticket confirms date 26/08/2014. 'Grain of Salt' is a restaurant name. Prof Mickael Gaborieau performed an organ concert.

pages.full_text: __Wednesday__ It's not raining but the weather didn't look hopeful. We had originally thought of spending 2 nights here, but with rain forecast for the afternoon, we moved out of the camp + set off on a bike ride. The campowner's wife said it wouldn't rain. We cycled N.W along the Brest-Nantes Canal. Then William rang to say his passport had been in the stolen Knapsack + he needed a new one for Majorca next week + wanted our passport details. We reached St. Andrew of the Rock (lovely- well-cared for church) + had coffee + cycled home, a bit of a gentle uphill, along the voie-verte - disused railway line. Lunch back in Malestroit. We didn't expect much when the mothy bar man said only sandwich or kebab, but the kebab turned out to be a big hamburger sandwich w/ chicken stew + salad - rather good.
pages.notes: Wednesday follows Tuesday (ticket dated 26/08/2014), so this is likely Wednesday 27/08/2014, but year is confirmed from left page ticket. 'St. Andrew of the Rock' is a church visited by bike along the Brest-Nantes Canal north-west of Malestroit. 'voie-verte' is the disused railway greenway. 'mothy' likely means 'mouthy' or could be a local expression. 'William' appears to be a family member (son?) whose passport was in a stolen knapsack.
